title: Application for Toyota | Business Logic Visualization

panels.title: Application for Toyota | Federation Business Logic

options.content:
# Application for Toyota | Autonomous Mobility Federation Logic

**Top control objective:** make autonomous mobility viable as an operating business by synchronizing demand, matching, dispatch, utilization, vehicle architecture, energy, node capacity, and PL.

**Core decision state:** `AUTHORIZED / HOLD / STOP`

**Federation scope:** Toyota HQ / Woven / Toyota Connected / Urban Operation / Service Hub / Fleet / Seneschal / Vehicle Architecture

## Trips Control

```text
Trips = Demand generation rate × Conversion rate × Matching rate × Dispatch success rate × Utilization
```

Trips are not fixed assumptions. They are produced by an integrated operating system.

| Factor | Definition |
|---|---|
| Demand generation rate | generated demand ÷ potential demand |
| Conversion rate | booking ÷ request |
| Matching rate | matched ÷ request |
| Dispatch success rate | completed dispatch ÷ dispatch request |
| Utilization | operating time ÷ total time |

## PL Control

```text
Revenue = Trips × Load × Price × Days
Cost = Energy + Operation + Vehicle + Infrastructure
Contribution = Revenue − Cost
```

| Lever | PL direction |
|---|---|
| Trips ↑ | Revenue ↑ |
| Load ↑ | Revenue efficiency ↑ |
| Price ↑ | Revenue per trip ↑ |
| Energy cost ↑ | Contribution ↓ |
| Vehicle / infrastructure cost ↑ | Payback worsens |
| Idle operation ↑ | Utilization ↓ / contribution ↓ |

## KPI Definition Matrix

| Layer | KPI | Formula |
|---|---|---|
| Demand | Demand generation rate | generated demand ÷ potential demand |
| Demand | Conversion rate | booking ÷ request |
| Dispatch | Matching rate | matched ÷ request |
| Dispatch | Dispatch success rate | completed dispatch ÷ dispatch request |
| Fleet | Utilization | operating time ÷ total time |
| Fleet | Fleet idle ratio | idle vehicles ÷ total vehicles |
| Experience | Waiting time | total waiting time ÷ trips |
| System | Latency | response time |
| Autonomy | Intervention rate | intervention ÷ trips |
| Energy | Energy cost per trip | energy cost ÷ trips |
| Energy | Supply stability | supplied energy ÷ required energy |
| Coverage | Matching coverage | served area ÷ total area |
| Node | Queue time | queue duration ÷ requests |
| Fleet | Rebalance rate | repositioned vehicles ÷ fleet |
| Operation | Schedule adherence | on-time ÷ total trips |
| Route | Route efficiency | optimal distance ÷ actual distance |
| Operation | Turnaround time | idle gap ÷ trips |
| Capacity | Capacity utilization | occupied seats ÷ total seats |
| Demand | Cancellation rate | canceled ÷ requests |
| Experience | ETA accuracy | on-time arrival ÷ trips |
| Operation | Service continuity | active hours ÷ total hours |
| Control | Control stability | spacing variance |
| Safety | Safety margin | control tolerance |
| Energy | Energy efficiency | output energy ÷ input energy |
| Infrastructure | Node utilization | used nodes ÷ total nodes |

## Constraint Gate

Execution is permitted only when constraints are within feasible operating range.

| Constraint | Control rule |
|---|---|
| Energy capacity | upper bound of system output |
| ODD | operational boundary |
| Latency | control feasibility |
| DDT | autonomy limit |
| Supply stability | energy availability |
| Safety margin | minimum operating tolerance |

```text
Constraint violation → STOP
If energy capacity is exceeded → STOP
If operation goes outside ODD → STOP
If latency exceeds feasibility → STOP
If DDT threshold is exceeded → STOP
If supply is insufficient → STOP dispatch
```

## AUTHORIZED / HOLD / STOP

| State | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|
| AUTHORIZED | all layers remain within operational and business thresholds | allow execution |
| HOLD | one or more layers are degraded but recoverable through reallocation | hold new authorization / reallocate |
| STOP | ODD, energy, fleet, safety, or business feasibility falls below minimum condition | stop execution |

**Decision owner:** Toyota Federation Layer  
**Execution owners:** Woven / Toyota Connected / Service Hub / Fleet / Seneschal

panels.title: If Control Logic | Demand / Matching / Dispatch

options.content:
## If Control Logic A

| If | Then |
|---|---|
| demand generation rate decreases | trigger demand generation |
| generated demand is below potential | trigger calendar-based demand |
| conversion rate decreases | reduce booking friction |
| booking rate is low | optimize decision interface |
| matching rate decreases | expand matching conditions |
| matching fails | expand spatial and temporal constraints |
| dispatch success rate decreases | reallocate fleet |
| dispatch fails | adjust routing and fleet allocation |
| coverage is low | expand service area |
| cancellations rise | improve matching quality |
| ETA accuracy drops | recalibrate ETA model |

panels.title: If Control Logic | Fleet / Operation / Energy

options.content:
## If Control Logic B

| If | Then |
|---|---|
| utilization decreases | eliminate idle through repositioning |
| utilization is low | enforce continuous operation |
| idle ratio increases | force vehicle redistribution |
| waiting time increases | improve dispatch responsiveness |
| queue time increases | increase dispatch frequency |
| rebalance rate is low | increase repositioning |
| schedule adherence drops | adjust routing buffers |
| route efficiency drops | re-optimize routing |
| turnaround increases | compress schedules |
| capacity is low | enable ride sharing |
| service continuity drops | extend operation window |
| energy cost exceeds limit | restrict trip generation |
| energy efficiency decreases | optimize energy usage |
| node utilization is low | reallocate infrastructure usage |

panels.title: If Control Logic | Autonomy / Safety / Control Loop

options.content:
## If Control Logic C

| If | Then |
|---|---|
| latency exceeds threshold | restrict execution |
| intervention increases | reduce autonomy scope |
| control stability degrades | adjust control parameters |
| safety margin is insufficient | restrict operation |
| predicted demand changes | recalculate planning |
| feasible allocation changes | update control decisions |
| system state changes | adjust execution |
| execution changes KPI | recalculate control loop |
| any state deviates | re-synchronize system |
| Trips increases | revenue increases proportionally |
| operation or energy increases | cost increases |
| revenue exceeds cost | contribution is positive |

## Vehicle Architecture → Business Impact

| Vehicle parameter | Direct effect | Federation propagation |
|---|---|---|
| Vehicle weight | payload ↓ / energy ↑ | vehicle weight → payload → fleet capacity → H2 demand → node queue → PL |
| Floor height | usability / loading efficiency ↓ | floor height → UX/load efficiency → demand conversion → PL |
| Energy system | refill time / uptime / range | energy system → refill time → node capacity → fleet availability → dispatch feasibility |
| MS-SOFC / SSB / H2 | base-load / traction / supply stability | energy architecture → uptime → trips/day → revenue |
| Swap body | turnaround / load flexibility | swap cycle → node queue → fleet availability → utilization |
| IWM / skateboard | low-floor / packaging | vehicle architecture → service fit → capacity utilization |

## Collapse Propagation

```text
ODD degradation
↓
usable fleet decreases
↓
dispatch capacity decreases
↓
queue and ETA worsen
↓
conversion and load factor decline
↓
PL deteriorates
↓
Federation state moves to HOLD / STOP
```

| Layer | Example state |
|---|---|
| ODD | HOLD |
| Fleet | DEGRADED |
| Queue | WARNING |
| Demand | RISK |
| PL | RISK |
| Federation Decision | HOLD |

## Toyota / Woven / Toyota Connected Federation

| Layer | Organization mapping | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Toyota HQ | Federation Layer | cross-company business control / KPI governance / AUTH-HOLD-STOP |
| Woven | Urban Operation Console | urban OS execution / ODD / node synchronization |
| Woven | Service Hub Console | node, energy, berth, queue, equipment constraint execution |
| Toyota Connected | Fleet Operations Console | fleet dispatch / utilization / mixed-load / service execution |
| Toyota Connected | Seneschal | user-side demand generation / calendar / execution interface |
| Vehicle development | Vehicle architecture | vehicle-energy-operating-business feasibility |

## Execution Loop

```text
Prediction → Planning → Control → Adjustment
```

| Step | Control meaning |
|---|---|
| Prediction | demand / constraint / fleet / energy forecast |
| Planning | route, dispatch, node, energy, capacity plan |
| Control | execution authorization and reallocation |
| Adjustment | KPI feedback and re-synchronization |

No asynchronous decision exists. Demand, fleet, energy, and time are resolved in a single control loop.

## System Synchronization

| Domain | Synchronization target |
|---|---|
| Demand | calendar-based intent / generated demand |
| Fleet | vehicle availability / utilization / dispatch state |
| Energy | supply stability / cost / node capacity |
| Time | ETA / schedule adherence / turnaround |
| Node | berth / queue / refill / equipment constraints |
| Business | revenue / cost / contribution / IRR readiness |
